concerns.analysis: Several meaningful charts and graphs from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output and have been replaced with bracketed placeholder text (e.g., `[Image: ...]`). For example, the maturity curve graph for Figure 1.14 is absent and replaced by a paragraph description.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Line graph showing a maturity curve (an S-shaped trend line). The x-axis represents Length (cm) from 22.5 to 72.5, and the y-axis represents Proportion Mature from 0.0 to 1.0. A legend indicates open circles are 'Observed' and the solid thick line is 'Predicted'.]

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a Table of Contents, List of Tables, and List of Figures with dotted leaders and page numbers. In the HTML output, these were extracted as tables retaining the raw page numbers. Since HTML is a continuous document without page boundaries, retaining these page numbers provides dead navigation and fails to adapt the document structure appropriately for the web format.
concerns.location: Pages 6-15 (Table of Contents, List of Tables, List of Figures)

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 16 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=16. Examples: pages 85-86: 7 cols (sex, age...); pages 86-87: 7 cols (sex, age...); pages 90-91: 3 cols (state, year...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 10 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=10. Examples: p135: "The number 135 is written in black text against a "; p146: "A solid white background with the number 146 in th"; p153: "A line graph showing the GLM-standardized index of". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 7 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=7, examples=['http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.201', 'https://www.st.nmfs.noaa.gov/Assets/Quality-Assurance/docume', 'http://nft.nefsc.noaa.gov/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://github.com/cmlegault/ASAPplots
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 8 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=8. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 239 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (173 truncated mid-sentence, 66 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p21: "...uals 1 over the denominator 1 plus e raised to the"; p128: "...the relationship between "Age" on the x-axis (0 to"; p129: "...nship between Age on the x-axis, ranging from 0 to"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=239 (truncated=173, placeholder=66, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p121(source:12cols,output:4cols), p226(source:48cols,output:16cols), p231(source:18cols,output:6cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p121(source:12cols,output:4cols), p226(source:48cols,output:16cols), p231(source:18cols,output:6cols)

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 172 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
Header/footer elements retained in output (422): ii; iii; iv
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=422. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
